senses.definition: the most fundamental qualities of a person or animal's character
a single person, particularly when considered as separate from a group, etc.

examples.x: Each individual in the team brings unique skills and perspectives to the project.
The psychologist emphasized the importance of understanding each individual's background and experiences.
In a democracy, every individual has the right to vote and participate in decision-making.

senses.definition: (of a person) behaving in an unkind, unfriendly, or ruthless
unable to survive, succeed, or stay healthy without someone or something
(of a person) managing one's life, work, and activities in an efficient way

examples.x: She is very organized and always finishes her work on time.
An organized person keeps their workspace neat and tidy.
He became more organized after learning how to use a planner.
The manager praised her for being the most organized member of the team.
He is so organized that he even plans his meals for the week.

senses.definition: enjoying other people's company and social interactions

examples.x: The outgoing student eagerly participated in group activities and made friends easily.
Her outgoing personality shone at social gatherings, where she effortlessly struck up conversations with strangers.
Despite being new to the neighborhood, the outgoing neighbor introduced himself to everyone on the block.
As an outgoing salesperson, he excelled in building relationships with clients and closing deals.
Her outgoing nature made her the life of the party, always bringing energy and laughter to social events.

senses.definition: to act in a specific way in order to make others believe that something is the case when actually it is not so

examples.x: He pretended to enjoy the meal, even though it didn't taste good, to avoid causing offense.
She pretended to be interested in the conversation to avoid hurting her friend's feelings.
Sometimes, pretending to be confident can help overcome nervousness in social situations.
The spy pretended to be a tourist while gathering information in a foreign country.

senses.definition: an essential and distinguishing attribute of something or someone

examples.x: Generosity is a defining quality of his character.
Their company's commitment to customer satisfaction is a key quality.
One quality of successful leaders is their ability to inspire others.
An important quality of a good book is its ability to captivate the reader from start to finish.

senses.definition: a notable feature or quality that defines or describes something

examples.x: One characteristic of good friends is loyalty.
Patience is a characteristic that helps in dealing with difficult situations.
One characteristic of her personality is her optimism.
Honesty is a characteristic that defines a good leader.

senses.definition: only relating or belonging to one person

examples.x: She kept her personal diary hidden under her bed.
His personal belongings were neatly packed in the suitcase.
The counselor offered personal advice tailored to each student's needs.
It's important to keep personal information confidential in a professional setting.
The artist's studio was filled with personal artwork and creative projects.

senses.definition: feeling calm and at ease without tension or stress

examples.x: After a long day at work, he enjoys taking a hot bath to feel relaxed and unwind.
Sitting by the fireplace, she felt relaxed as she listened to the crackling of the flames.
The serene beach and gentle waves made her feel relaxed and carefree.
With a good book in hand and a cup of tea, he spent the afternoon feeling relaxed in his favorite armchair.
Breathing deeply and focusing on the present moment helps to promote a relaxed state of mind.

senses.definition: (of a person) not stressed, and free from worries

examples.x: Despite the stressful situation, she remained calm and easy, effortlessly navigating the challenges.
His easy demeanor and laid-back attitude made him a pleasure to be around.
Even in difficult circumstances, her easy charm and affability never wavered.
Despite the pressure, she remained easy and relaxed, always finding a way to lighten the mood.
His easy personality makes him popular with everyone.

senses.definition: able to be trusted to perform consistently well and meet expectations

examples.x: He's reliable, always delivering on his promises and consistently producing quality work.
They've proven to be reliable partners in every project we've done.
Despite challenges, the reliable employee consistently meets deadlines and exceeds expectations.
The car is old but still reliable for long drives.
The reliable product has a reputation for durability and performance.

senses.definition: deeply knowledgeable and experienced and capable of giving good advice or making good decisions

examples.x: Grandparents are often considered wise because of their life experiences and wisdom.
The wise leader carefully considers all options before making decisions for the team.
Wise mentors can provide valuable guidance to those seeking to learn and grow.
The wise elder shared her wisdom with the younger generations, offering valuable insights into life.
Heeding the warnings of wise elders can help avoid potential pitfalls and regrets in life.

senses.definition: not fast at learning or understanding things
to deceive a person so that they do what one wants

examples.x: He tricked his brother into giving him the last piece of cake by pretending it was his birthday.
The salesman tricked customers into buying unnecessary products by using high-pressure tactics.
She tricked her friend into believing they had won the lottery as a prank.
The con artist tricked the elderly couple into giving them their life savings with promises of fake investments.
Be wary of emails that attempt to trick you into revealing personal information or clicking on malicious links.

senses.definition: easily influenced by others and lacking the ability or will to uphold one's decisions, beliefs, etc.

examples.x: Sarah's weak character was evident in her tendency to change her mind frequently, unable to stand firm in her decisions.
The weak leader's indecisiveness caused confusion among the team, leading to a lack of direction.
The weak student succumbed to peer pressure, abandoning his principles to fit in with the crowd.
Despite his initial objections, John was quit weak and got easily swayed by his friends' opinions.

senses.definition: (of a person) behaving in a way that is unkind or cruel

examples.x: The mean girl spread rumors about her classmates to make herself feel superior.
He showed his mean streak by mocking his coworker's appearance behind their back.
The mean boss berated employees for minor mistakes, creating a toxic work environment.
He regretted his mean behavior towards his younger sibling, realizing it was unfair.
The mean neighbor complained about trivial matters just to cause trouble.

senses.definition: behaving in a way that is immature or typical of a child

examples.x: Instead of resolving their issues maturely, they resorted to childish name-calling and insults.
The teenager's constant giggling during the serious discussion was seen as childish by the adults.
Making faces at the teacher behind her back was a childish way of expressing discontent with the lesson.
His refusal to share his toys with his younger sibling was seen as selfish and childish.
The childish prank of hiding someone's belongings may seem harmless, but it can cause frustration and inconvenience.

senses.definition: showing firm and constant support to a person, organization, cause, or belief

examples.x: The loyal friend stood by his side through thick and thin, offering unwavering support.
Despite offers from competing companies, he remained loyal to his employer, valuing the relationships he had built over the years.
Her loyal dedication to the cause inspired others to join the movement and work towards positive change.
The loyal fan faithfully attended every game, cheering enthusiastically for their team win or lose.
The loyal companion never wavered in their devotion to their owner, offering unconditional love and companionship.

senses.definition: having a straightforward and honest attitude

examples.x: She has an open attitude and is always willing to discuss any issues that arise.
Her open demeanor encouraged others to trust her easily.
They appreciated her open response when asked about the challenges she faced.
He was open about his past experiences, even the difficult ones.
She gave an open and honest opinion about the proposal during the meeting.

senses.definition: (of a person) dishonest, cruel, and taking pleasure in causing harm or suffering to others

examples.x: The evil villain cackled with delight as he watched the hero struggle.
The evil dictator showed no remorse as he oppressed his people.
The evil witch cast spells on innocent villagers for her own amusement.
His evil deeds haunted him, but he found perverse pleasure in them nonetheless.
The evil mastermind plotted to destroy the city and relished the chaos it would cause.

senses.definition: able to be relied on and trusted

examples.x: She is a responsible pet owner, ensuring her animals are well-cared for and loved.
He is a responsible employee, always completing his assignments on time and with accuracy.
The company chose him for the promotion because of his track record of being a responsible and dedicated employee.
She was entrusted with a leadership role because of her reputation for being responsible and reliable.
He's a responsible team captain, leading by example and motivating his teammates to excel.

senses.definition: (of a person) having an enigmatic or puzzling quality, often suggesting hidden motives or characteristics

examples.x: The new neighbor was mysterious, rarely seen and hardly speaking to anyone.
She remained mysterious, revealing little about her past to those around her.
The mysterious stranger arrived at the party without anyone knowing who he was.
The mysterious woman in the café seemed to be watching everyone closely.
He was a mysterious figure, rarely speaking but always observing.

senses.definition: having or displaying a strong will to achieve a goal despite the challenges or obstacles

examples.x: She was determined to finish the marathon, despite the pain in her legs.
He remained determined to learn a new language, practicing every day.
The team was determined to win the championship, training rigorously.
Despite setbacks, she remained determined to pursue her dream career.
Her determined spirit inspired everyone around her to work harder.

senses.definition: a feeling of being uneasy, troubled, or worried about something such as problem, threat, uncertainty, etc.

examples.x: Her voice trembled with concern as she asked about his recent accident.
The doctor expressed concern about the unusual rash on the child's skin.
Despite their outward confidence, a flicker of concern betrayed their true feelings.
The environmental group voiced their concern about the proposed construction project.

senses.definition: to be thankful for something

examples.x: She regularly appreciates the support of her friends during challenging times.
The employees often appreciate the flexible work hours.
Yesterday, he warmly appreciated the thoughtful birthday gift from his family.
The community appreciated the efforts of volunteers who cleaned up the park.
Thank you, I appreciate your kind words of encouragement.
